items.title: Spurs' Simons to miss rest of season and World Cup
items.summary: Tottenham and Netherlands attacking midfielder Xavi Simons is "heartbroken" to have injured his anterior cruciate ligament.

items.title: 'Feyenoord moet Ueda juist niet verkopen'
items.summary: Feyenoord deed zaterdag goede zaken in de strijd om plek twee in de Eredivisie door met 3-1 te winnen van FC Groningen. Ayase Ueda was de grote man met twee treffers en bracht zijn seizoenstotaal daarmee op 25. Waar ligt de toekomst van de Japanse spits? Lentin Goodijk en Marco Timmer bespreken het in Rondje Eredivisie .

items.title: KNVB wijst scheidsrechter aan voor Eredivisie-topper Ajax - PSV
items.summary: Allard Lindhout is door de KNVB aangesteld als scheidsrechter voor de topper tussen Ajax en PSV van zaterdagavond. De aftrap in de Johan Cruijff ArenA is om 20.00 uur.

items.title: Real Madrid heeft onderzoeksresultaten van blessure Mbappé binnen
items.summary: Kylian Mbappé heeft in de uitwedstrijd tegen Real Betis een blessure aan een van zijn hamstringspieren in zijn linkerbovenbeen opgelopen. Dat heeft Real Madrid bevestigd. De club wil naar buiten toe niet prijsgeven wanneer de 27-jarige Fransman wordt terugverwacht.

items.title: Farioli gaat kampioenswedstrijd spelen: gekte bij Porto-fans en run op kaarten
items.summary: Francesco Farioli staat met FC Porto op de drempel van de Portugese landstitel. Na de overwinning op Estrela Amadora en het puntenverlies van Sporting Portugal kan de club komende speelronde al kampioen worden.

items.title: Scott Parker’s future as Burnley manager in the balance after relegation to Championship
items.summary: Manager to hold talks with Burnley’s chair, Alan Pace Parker may seek amicable parting with year on his deal Scott Parker’s future as the Burnley manager is in the balance after the club’s relegation, with discussions between the 45-year-old and Alan Pace, the chair, expected to determine whether he will remain in place for next season. No decision has been made and it is understood that Parker is unsure whether to continue and may seek an amicable parting with one year on his contract. Continue reading...

items.title: Blessurecrisis Tottenham roept vragen op: 'Kan niet alleen pech zijn'
items.summary: Functioneren de specialisten in de staf van Tottenham Hotspur die de spelers zo fit mogelijk moeten krijgen en houden wel optimaal? Voormalig Spurs-manager Tim Sherwood vraagt zich dat hardop af. Bij Sky Sports wordt besproken dat de zware blessure van Xavi Simons niet op zichzelf staat.

items.title: 🎥 Nicolas Raskin buteur...pour rien : fameuse sensation dans les Playoffs écossais
items.summary: Les Glasgow Rangers a bien mal débuté ses Playoffs, s'inclinant 2-3 contre Motherwell. Nicolas Raskin a pourtant donné de sa personne pour garder son équipe dans le match.

items.title: Bommetje: Europees topvoetbal dreigt deels achter betaalmuur te verdwijnen in België
items.summary: Het televisielandschap in Europa blijft razendsnel veranderen. En alles wijst erop dat die evolutie alleen maar sneller zal gaan. Disney+ zou binnenkort namelijk Champions League-wedstrijden uitzenden in ons land.

items.title: La fin de la Coupe d'Europe en clair en Belgique ? Disney+ sur le point de lancer la révolution
items.summary: Le paysage télévisuel continue à changer à grande vitesse en Europe. Et cela devrait continuer à s'accélérer.

items.title: Could a Premier League club lose their European place once again?
items.summary: After Crystal Palace's issues last season, could a Premier League club lose their European place once again because of dual ownership issues?

items.title: Anderlecht a-t-il trouvé la clé pour la finale de Coupe ? "Ils ont montré comment il faut jouer l'Union"
items.summary: La défaite 1-3 contre l'Union Saint-Gilloise est un nouveau coup dur pour Anderlecht. Mais elle a offert quelques renseignements aux Mauves en prévision de la finale de la Coupe.

items.title: Missy Bo Kearns says Aston Villa doctors probably saved her life after miscarriage
items.summary: Midfielder went through ‘four days of hell in hospital’ Hopes her talking will stop others ‘suffering in silence’ Missy Bo Kearns has described experiencing “a different type of grief” after having a miscarriage last month. The Aston Villa and England midfielder had announced her pregnancy just over two weeks before sharing the tragic loss of her and her partner Liam Walsh’s baby. Speaking to ITV News, the 25-year-old said she thought she was experiencing symptoms from the pregnancy on 18 March when she was shaking and had a temperature of 42C but the Aston Villa team doctor, Dr Jodie Blackadder-Weinstein, told her she needed to call Walsh and get to hospital. Continue reading...

items.title: Premier League and FA Cup semi-finals: 10 talking points from the weekend’s action
items.summary: Sánchez plays long game for McFarlane, Southampton can take heart, Arteta tries to gain edge and Isak will come good One moment from their FA Cup semi-final to Chelsea will haunt Leeds. When Tosin Adarabioyo stretched for a through ball and couldn’t quite get there, quarter of an hour in, everything seemed to slow down. There was Brenden Aaronson with just Robert Sánchez to beat, with the chance to put Leeds ahead against a side that hadn’t scored in five Premier League games and had seemingly lost all confidence. Even at the time it felt a huge moment. The US international didn’t do much wrong, but Sánchez made a fine save with his foot. That, it turned out, was the game. There were other opportunities – most notably Anton Stach’s drive that Sánchez saved spectacularly and the Dominic Calvert-Lewin header just after that, aimed straight at the keeper. They came after Chelsea had taken the lead and the emotional tone was set, though. Sometimes one chance can define a game. Jonathan Wilson FA Cup semi-final report: Chelsea 1-0 Leeds Jonathan Wilson: Chelsea chaos theory delivers another trophy chance FA Cup semi-final report: Manchester City 2-1 Southampton Continue reading...

items.title: One weekend, two games and 7,140 sq metres of grass: a week with the Wembley ground staff
items.summary: From preparation to game mode, the upkeep of Wembley’s pitch is carried out to an astonishing level of perfectionism Karl Standley and his assistant Cameron Hutcheon have gathered in their usual spot in the south-west corner of Wembley Stadium clutching hot cups of tea. Standley is a coffee devotee but on matchdays, as a nod to his mum who enjoys a brew whatever the temperature, he mixes things up. After every kick-off the pair gaze out at 7,140 sq metres of glistening green perfection like lions surveying their savannah. Every thinkable controllable has been controlled and, for a short time at least, the teams – this time Manchester City and Southampton – have dual custody of the Wembley pitch. Continue reading...

items.title: Making history and facing Neymar - Lingard on life in Brazil
items.summary: In his first major interview since moving to Brazil, Jesse Lingard talks to the BBC about his new life, and Manchester United.

items.title: 🎥 KV Mechelen zoekt zich suf naar verklaringen: "Dan hebben we een groot probleem"
items.summary: Wie de vier opeenvolgende nederlagen van KV Mechelen analyseert, komt al snel tot een vaststelling. De huidige nummer 6 in de stand speelde telkens een degelijke tot goede eerste helft, maar een véél mindere tweede helft. Ligt de verklaring op fysiek of mentaal gebied?

items.title: Xavi Simons ruled out for rest of season and World Cup with ruptured ACL
items.summary: ‘Heartbroken’ Tottenham midfielder injured at Wolves Netherlands star faces eight months on the sidelines Xavi Simons has ruptured his anterior cruciate ligament and will be out for around eight months. The Tottenham midfielder suffered the injury in his team’s 1-0 win at Wolves on Saturday and will be unavailable for the remainder of the club’s Premier League survival fight. His devastation has been compounded by the knowledge that he will not be able to play for the Netherlands at the World Cup finals this summer. Simons was stretchered off at Molineux in the 63rd minute after twisting his knee in the turf as he chased a ball towards the byline. It is a terrible blow for him and the club, whose new manager, Roberto De Zerbi, was counting on the 23-year-old’s creativity in the battle against relegation. Despite the victory over Wolves, which was Spurs’ first in 16 league games, they remain 18th in the table, two points behind 17th-placed West Ham with four matches to play. Continue reading...

items.title: Nieuwe tegenvaller voor Kompany in aanloop naar kraker tegen PSG: toptalent niet op tijd fit
items.summary: Bayern München trekt dinsdag gehavend naar PSG voor de heenmatch in de halve finales van de Champions League. Vincent Kompany moet niet alleen zelf geschorst toekijken, maar mist ook Lennart Karl.

items.title: "Je signerai dans un club pour jouer numéro neuf" : Thomas Meunier vers une reconversion en fin de carrière ?
items.summary: Thomas Meunier a pour objectif de terminer sa carrière comme numéro neuf. Il ne s'en est une nouvelle fois pas caché.

items.title: Dit gaat gevolgen hebben! Malaise bij OH Leuven werkt zwaar op de zenuwen
items.summary: De nederlaag van OH Leuven tegen Sporting Charleroi heeft diepe sporen nagelaten. Coach Felice Mazzu was bijzonder scherp na afloop: "Ik ben beschaamd voor het publiek en voor de club."

items.title: Un ancien espoir du Standard profite de la fin de saison de la RAAL : "Certains joueurs ne veulent pas jouer"
items.summary: La RAAL n'a plus rien à perdre en cette fin de saison. Noah Makembo fait partie de ces jeunes appelés à se montrer.

items.title: Meer nieuws over Kos Karetsas die blessure opliep tegen Standard
items.summary: KRC Genk hield tegen Standard niet alleen een wrang punt over, maar vreesde ook even voor een zware blessure bij Kos Karetsas. Intussen kregen de Limburgers daarover toch al een eerste opluchting.

items.title: 'Four days of hell in hospital' - Kearns on miscarriage
items.summary: Aston Villa and England midfielder Missy Bo Kearns says she spent "four days of hell in hospital" after suffering a miscarriage and contracting sepsis.

items.title: Hire, fire, final - Chelsea's player power may be ugly, but it wins
items.summary: Chelsea are in another FA Cup final - but only after their players showed up in a way they were no longer doing for Liam Rosenior, writes Phil McNulty.

items.title: Trump misses out on The Open, as Royal Lytham picked as venue for 2028
items.summary: Donald Trump's Turnberry Golf Club will not host The Open Championship in the immediate future after Royal Lytham & St Annes Golf Club was named as host of The 156th Open in 2028.

items.summary: Sky Sports pundits Gary Neville and Paul Merson have backed Eddie Howe at Newcastle despite a poor recent run of results that has taken the Magpies from the European football picture to the bottom half of the Premier League table.

items.title: Who will make England's Women's T20 World Cup squad?
items.summary: England Women head coach Charlotte Edwards will announce her squad for the T20 World Cup on Tuesday and she has some difficult decisions to make.
